title: The One Where Ross Finds Out

lines.text: Originally written by Michael Borkow
Monica and Rachel's apartment. Everyone is sitting at the couches, Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: OK, what is it about me? Do I not look fun enough? Is there something. . . repellant. . . about me?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: So, how was the party?

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: Well it couldn't have been worse. A woman literally passed through me. OK, so what is it, am I hideously unattractive?

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: No, you are not, you are very attractive. You know what, I go through the exact same thing. Every time I put on a little weight, I start questioning everyting.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: Woah, woah, I've put on a little weight?

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: No, not wieght... y'know, more like insulation.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: Chandler, I'm unemployed and in dire need of a project. Ya wanna work out? I can remake you.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: Oh, you know, I would, but that might get in the way of my lying around time.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: Please.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: C'mon. Let her. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: Alright, OK, alright. But if we put on spandex and my boobs are bigger than yours, I'm goin' home.

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: Your boobs are fine. Look, I never should have said anything. Come here. Come here. [hugs Chandler but holds her hands apart behind his back] Oh, can't make.... hands... meet....
OPENING TITLES
Hallway between the apartments. Chandler comes out wearing spandex, jogging in place. Monica is there.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: OK, let's do it. [Monica looks at him funny] What?

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: Nothing, just never seen you in little stretchy pants before.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: And we're changing. [jogs back in his apartment]
[Cut to the city street. Monica and Chandler are jogging. Chandler is lagging behind so he hops in a cab and takes off, leaving Monica behind]
Back in Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler is doing situps.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: C'mon give me five more. Five more.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: [weakly] No.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: Five more and I'll flash you.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: One. . . two. . . two and a half. OK, just show me one of them.
Central Perk. Chandler and Joey are sitting on the couch. Rachel is working.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: [slowly lifts coffee cup to his mouth] Ow, ow, ow, ow, ow, ow, ow. [slowly sets the cup back down] Ow, ow, ow, ow, ow. [Joey intercepts the cup and puts it down for him]. She's insane, the woman is insane. It's before work, it's after work, it's during work. She's got me doing butt clenches at my desk. And now, they won't bring me my mail anymore.
[Phoebe enters.]

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Hey Phoebs, how'd it go with Scott last night?

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: Oh, um, it was nice. Took him to a romantic restraunt, ordered champagne, nice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The guy still won't put out, huh?

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: Nope. Zilch, nothin', uh-uh.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Sorry Phoebs.

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: Look, I, y'know, I don't mind taking it slow, I like him a lot, y'know he's really interesting and he's really sweet and why won't he give it up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe he, uhh... drives his car on the other side of the road, if ya know what I mean.

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: No, whad'ya mean? He's not British.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe he's. . . gay.

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: Oohh, um, no, I don't think that's the problem. 'Cause we went, um, dancing the other night and the way he held me so close, and the way he was looking into my eyes I just like... definitely felt something.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Yeah, but how much can you tell from a look?

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: No, I felt it on my hip. You could tell.
[Monica enters.]

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: [to Chandler] Yo, Bing. Racquetball in 15 minutes.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: Joey, be a pal. Lift up my hand and smack her with it.

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: [seeing Ross kissing Julie outside the window] Ooh, oh, Rachel, don't look.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: What? [looks, feigns indifference] C'mon you guys, I don't care, I have a date tonight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Woah, woah, woah, you have a date?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Yeah, Monica's settin' me up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But uh, uh, what about uh, Ross and uh. . .?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Oh what, my whole insane jealousy thing? Well, y'know, as much fun as that was, I've decided to opt for sanity.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: So you really OK about all this?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Oh yeah, c'mon, I'm movin' on. He can press her up against that window as much as he wants. For all I care, he can throw her through the damn thing.
[Ross and Julie enter.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi guys.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, Monica, I figured I'd come by tomorrow morning and pick up Fluffy's old cat toy, OK?

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: Only if you say his full name.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: [reluctantly] Can I come over tomorrow and pick up Fluffy Meowington's cat toy.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: [to Ross] You're getting a cat?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, actually, we're getting a cat.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Together?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh huh.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Both of you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yep.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Together.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Yeah, we figure it'll live with Ross half the time, and with me half the time.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Ohh, well, isn't that just lovely. That's something the two of you will be able to enjoy for a really, really, really, really, really long time.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hopefully.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Well. [looks at watch] Woah, look at that! I gotta go, I gotta date. With a man. Um, OK, you guys have a really, uh, have a really good night and you two have a, uh, have a, uh, really good cat. [she leaves carrying her tray then comes back in] OK, we're not supposed to take these when we leave.
A nice restraunt. Rachel is on her date with Michael (MICH).

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: I don't know if Monica told you but this is the first date I've gone on since my divorce so, if I seem a little nervous, I am.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: [distracted] How long do cats live?

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: [confused] I'm sorry?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Cats, how long do they live figuring you don't... y'know, throw 'em under a bus or something?

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: Um, maybe 15, 16 years.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: That's just great. [she picks up her champagne and starts drinking]

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: Um, cheers.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Oh, right, clink. [downs her glass]

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: Monica told you I was cuter that this, didn't she?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Oh, no, Michael, it's not you. I'm sorry, it's just, it's this thing. It's probably not as bad as it sounds but this friend of mine is, is getting a cat with his girlfriend.

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: Oh, that does sound. . .Ahh.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: I mean he just started going out with her.

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: Is this guy, uhh, an old boyfriend?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Ah, hah-hah-hah-ho, yeah, he wishes. Oh, I'm sorry, look at me. OK, Michael, let's talk about you.

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: OK, OK. So, you ever get a pet with a girlfriend?
Central Perk. Phoebe and Joey are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: So, I figured it out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: Why Scott doesn't want to sleep with me. It's 'cause I'm not sexy enough.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Phoebe, that's crazy. When I first met you, you know what I said to Chandler? I said, "Excellent butt, great rack."

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: Really? That's so sweet. I mean, I'm officially offended but, sweet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Phoebs look, if you want to know what the deal is, you're just gonna have to ask him.

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: You're right, you're right. Ah, you are so yumm. [they hug]

[Outside the window, Monica and Chandler jog up. Monica playfully pushes him. They start puching and slapping harder and harder until Monica pushes him down. Chandler stands up, with a serious expression, and chases her away.]
Back in the restraunt. Rachel pours the last of the champange bottle in her glass.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: [obviously drunk] I mean, it's a cat, y'know, it's a cat. Why can't they get one of those bugs, y'know, one of those fruitflies, those things that live for like a day or something? [belligerently] What're they called, what're they called, what're they called?

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: Fruitflies?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Yes! Thank you.
[The waiter comes to the table.]

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: So, would you like any dessert?

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: No! No dessert, just a check, please.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Oh, you're not having fun, are you?

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: No, no, I am, but only because for the last hour and a half I've been playing the movie Diner in my head.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Oh, look at me, look at me. Oh, I'm on a date with a really great guy, all I can think about is Ross and his cat and his... Julie. I just want to get over him. gosh, why can't I do that?

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: Oy. Look, I've been through a divorce, trust me you're gonna be fine. You just can't see it now because you haven't had any closure.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Yeah! Closure. That's what it is, that's what I need. God, you're brilliant! Why didn't I think of that? How do I get that?

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: Well, you know, there's no one way really, it's just, you know, whatever it takes so that you can finally say to him, "I'm over you."

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Closure, that's what it is. Closure. [she looks around the restaurant, spotting a guy with a cellular phone] Hello, excuse me. Excuse me, hel. . . woo [she almost falls out of her chair]

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Hang on.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Hello, excuse me.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: What.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Hi, I'm sorry, I need to borrow your phone for just one minute.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: I'm talkin'!

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: I can see that. I... just one phone call, I'll be very quick, I'll even pay for it myself. [man is still reluctant] OK, you're bein' a little weird about your phone.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Alright, fine. [on the phone] I'll call you back. [hands the phone to her]

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Thank you. OK. [dials] [to Michael] Machine. Just waiting for the beep.

lines.speaker: Mich
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: [on phone] Ross, hi, it's Rachel. I'm just calling to say that um, everything's fine and I'm really happy for you and your cat who, by the way, I think you should name Michael. And, you know, ya see there I'm thinking of names so obviously, I am over you. I am over you and that, my friend, is what they call closure. [hangs up and tosses phone in the ice bucket]
COMMERCIAL BREAK
Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler is answering the door in his robe.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: No, no, no, no, no, no [opens door to Monica] No. Monica, it's Sunday morning. I'm not running on a Sunday.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: Because it's Sunday. It's God's day.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: OK, if you say stop, then we stop.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: OK, stop.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: No, c'mon, we can't stop, c'mon, we've got three more pounds to go. I am the energy train and you are on board. Woo-woo, woo-woo, woo-woo [Chandler walks out of the apartment, leaving Monica] Woo.
Rachel and Monica's apartment. Rachel is taking asprin. Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Rach.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Ahhhh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. And how was the date?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Umm, I think there was a restaurant... I know there was wine. . .
[Rachel looks at Ross as though she remembers something, but can't place what it is.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow, well uh, uh, actually, Julie's downstairs getting a cab, I just need the cat toy, did Monica say. . . What? Why, why are you looking at me like that?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: I don't know, I, I feel like I had a dream about you last night but I, I don't remember.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: OK. Oh, oh, oh. [runs over and picks up the cat toy]

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Did we speak on the phone last night? Did you call me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I stayed at Julie's last night.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, actually I haven't even been home yet. Do you mind if I check my messages?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Oh yeah, go ahead. [Rachel walks in her room. Ross picks up the phone and dials his machine to check his messages.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, I got a message from you. [pauses] Who's Michael?
[Rachel comes out of her room, suddenly she remembers leaving the message.]

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Oh my God. Oh my God Ross, no, hang up the phone, give me the phone Ross, give me the phone, give me the phone, give me the. . . [jumps the couch and lands on Ross's back, finally getting the phone from him. Ross has a confused expression on his face.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're over me?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Ohhhhhhhh God. [climbs off his back]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha... you're uh, you're, you're over me?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Ohh, ohh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: When, when were you... under me? Rach. Rachel do you, I mean, were you, uh. . . What?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Ohh, OK, OK, OK, well, basically, lately, I've uh, I've uh, sort of had feelings for you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You've had feelings for me?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Yeah, what, so? You had feelings for me first.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Woah. Huh. You know about my, I mean, you know I had... you know?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Chandler told me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chandler. When did he... when did he... when did he?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: When you were in China.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: China.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Meeting Julie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Julie. Julie. That. Oh God. Julie, right. OK, I need to lie down. No, ya know, I'm gonna stand. I'm gonna stand, I'm gonna walk, I'm walkin' and I am standing. OK so you uh, and now wha... and now, now, now you're over me?

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Are you over me?
[A moment of silence.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: [doorbell buzzes] That's, that's Julie. Ju... Julie, Julie. [talks on intercom] Hi Julie.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: [over intercom] Hi honey, I've got a cab waiting.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: [perky] I'll be right down.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Wait, so, you're going?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, OK, I uh, I have to. I can't deal with this right now. I mean, I've uh, y'know, I've got a cab, I've got a girlfriend, I'm... I'm gonna go get a cat.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: OK, OK.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Cat. [leaves]
Chandler and Joey's apartment. Joey is watching a rabbi play an electric guitar on TV. Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: Hey Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Phoebs.

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: How come you're watching a rabbi play electric guitar?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't find the remote. [Phoebe turns off the TV] Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: So, Scott asked me to come over for lunch today and I did.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: And we did.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right Phoebs, way to go.

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: Yay me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, so how did it happen?

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: Well, I finally took your advice and asked him what was going on.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And what did he say?

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: He said that, um, he understands how sex can be like, a very emotional thing for a woman and he was just afraid that I was gonna get all, y'know, like, 'ohh, is he gonna call me the next day' and, y'know, 'where is this going' and, ya know, blah-la-la-la-la. So he said he wanted to hold off until he was prepared to be really serious.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: Yeah, so I said, "OK, relax please," y'know, I mean, sex can be just about two people right there in the moment, y'know, it's, if he wants to see me again he can call and if not, that's fine too. So after a looooot of talking. . . I convinced him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Let me get this straight. He got you to beg to sleep with him, he got you to say he never has to call you again, and he got you thinking this was a great idea.

lines.speaker: Phoe
lines.text: Um-hum.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This man is my God.
Central Perk. Rachel is closing up and Ross comes in. Get your Kleenex.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I didn't get a cat.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Oh, that's um, interesting.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no it's not interesting. OK, it's very, very not interesting. In fact it's actually 100 percent completely opposite of interesting.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Alright, I got it Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You had no right to tell me you ever had feelings for me.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: [hurt] What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I was doing great with Julie before I found out about you.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Hey, I was doin' great before I found out about you. You think it's easy for me to see you with Julie?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then you should have said something before I met her.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: I didn't know then. And how come you never said anything to me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There was never a good time.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Right, you, you only had a year. We only hung out every night.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not, not, not every night. You know, and... and it's not like I didn't try, Rachel, but things got in the way, y'know? Like, like Italian guys or ex-fiances or, or, or Italian guys.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Hey, there was one Italian guy, OK, and do you even have a point?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The point is I... I don't need this right now, OK. It, it's too late, I'm with somebody else, I'm happy. This ship has sailed.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Yeah, what're you saying, you just sort of put away feelings or whatever the hell it was you felt for me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I've been doin' it since the ninth grade, I've gotten pretty damn good at it.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Alright, fine, you go ahead and you do that, alright Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: 'Cause I don't need your stupid ship.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: Good. [Ross leaves]
[Rachel gets up and opens the door, yelling after him.]

lines.speaker: Rach
lines.text: And ya know what, now I've got closure.

[Rachel slams the door and locks it. She sits down, visibly upset. She puts her head in her hands and begins to cry. Ross comes back and is standing outside the window. When Rachel regroups and gets back up to finish closing, she sees him. She smiles. She goes to open the door and can't get the lock undone.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Try the bottom one.
[She opens the door and they kiss.]
Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler answers the door to find Monica.
CLOSING CREDITS

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: Monica, it's 6:30 in the morning. We're not working out, it's over.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: No way, with one pound to go, c'mon. We're workin', we're movin', we're in the zone we're groovin'.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: OK, I don't, I don't mind the last pound. OK, in fact I kind of like the last pound. OK, so don't make me do anything that I'll regret.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: Ooh, what'cha gonna do, fat boy, huh? What?

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: Nothing, except tell you, uh, I think it's wonderful how much energy you have.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: Well, thanks.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: I mean, especially considering how tough it's been for you to find work.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: Well, you know.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: You know, I mean, you can't tell your parents you were fired because they'd be disappointed.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: [sad] Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: And it's not as if you have a boyfriend's shoulder to cry on.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: Well no, but um.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: I mean, if it were me, I think I'd have difficulty just getting out of bed at all.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: Y'know, I try to stay positive. . .

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: So, you feel like goin' for a run?

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: Because, you know, you don't have to. If you want, you could just take a nap right here.

lines.speaker: Mnca
lines.text: OK. Just for a little while.

lines.speaker: Chan
lines.text: OK. [Puts an afghan over her and dances into his room]
END
